# t21.3 - Feature Parity Sign-off Rerun After Evidence Bundle Completion

## Verdict

FAIL - PM sign-off is still not approved.

`t21.2.2` materially improves the parity evidence bundle. It closes the seeded DB2 regressions that were still open in `t21.1`: the primitive JAX-RS echo endpoints now respond on the authoritative lane, the seeded scenario driver again traverses the preserved account/quote/portfolio/order/logout/register surfaces, and the operator reset/rebuild flows again return the expected runtime pages. Those closures move several previously open requirements to evidenced.

PM sign-off still cannot pass, because the approved parity inventory from `t3` keeps every directly reachable docs, alternate-page, and primitive surface in scope, and the completed evidence bundle still does not prove those families exhaustively. The remaining proof is still representative for those families rather than inventory-complete.

## Inputs Reviewed

- `t3-pm.md`
- `t21.1-pm.md`
- `t20.5-tester.md`
- `t21.2.2-tester.md`
- `e2e/tests/daytrader-smoke.spec.ts`
- `src/test/java/com/ibm/websphere/samples/daytrader/integration/journeys/DayTraderJourneyIntegrationTest.java`
- `src/test/java/com/ibm/websphere/samples/daytrader/integration/streaming/DayTraderStreamingIntegrationTest.java`
- `src/test/java/com/ibm/websphere/samples/daytrader/web/mvc/TradeScenarioControllerTest.java`

## Evidence Delta Since t21.1

- Closed: seeded DB2 operator parity that was still open in `t21.1`.
  - `t21.2.2` proves `config?action=resetTrade` returns the preserved run-stats surface with the expected success message.
  - `t21.2.2` proves authenticated `config?action=buildDBTables` now returns `200 OK` and re-renders the preserved config surface.
- Closed: seeded DB2 scenario-driver parity that was still open in `t21.1`.
  - `t21.2.2` proves runtime actions `l`, `h`, `a`, `u`, `q`, `p`, `b`, `s`, `o`, and `r` again land on the preserved home, account, quote, portfolio, order, logout, and register surfaces.
- Closed: seeded DB2 primitive JAX-RS parity that was still open in `t21.1`.
  - `t21.2.2` proves `/jaxrs/sync/echoText`, `/echoJSON`, and `/echoXML` all return `200` with the expected echoed payloads.
- Not closed: full parity proof for the broader directly reachable docs, alternate-page, and primitive inventories listed in `t3`.
- Not closed: the remaining user-visible behavioral gaps that still lack explicit sign-off evidence.

## Remaining Sign-off Findings

### CRITICAL - Full parity proof is still missing for the directly reachable documentation, alternate-page, and broader primitive inventories

The completed evidence bundle still does not account for the full surface families that `t3` keeps in scope. The browser suite covers only one primitive servlet path, and the running-server integration coverage still proves only one alternate JSF entry (`welcome.faces`) plus one primitive servlet reachability check. No completed-evidence artifact proves the primitive launcher page itself, the docs-linked pages, the image-based JSP variants, the broader directly reachable JSF/XHTML page set, the primitive WebSocket pages, or the rest of the linked primitive launcher destinations.

Impacted requirements: `REQ-001`, `REQ-026`, `REQ-027`, `REQ-028`, `REQ-029`.

### HIGH - Several user-visible behaviors remain only partially evidenced in the final sign-off bundle

The final evidence bundle still does not add focused proof for invalid-login messaging, quote deep-link behavior, empty-portfolio rendering, show-all-orders expansion, successful profile update, market-summary page rendering, completed-order alerts across trading pages, SSE follow-up update behavior, the market-summary WebSocket browser-page behavior, or the full anonymous-access rule that leaves only login and registration available to unauthenticated users.

Impacted requirements: `REQ-004`, `REQ-008`, `REQ-012`, `REQ-013`, `REQ-014`, `REQ-016`, `REQ-017`, `REQ-023`, `REQ-024`, `REQ-030`.

## Requirement Status Delta

| Area | Status after `t21.3` review | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Authoritative DB2-backed infrastructure lane | Closed | `t20.5` already closed the real-infra blocker from earlier PM sign-off reruns. |
| Seeded operator reset/rebuild actions | Closed | `t21.2.2` proves `resetTrade` and `buildDBTables` on the seeded DB2 lane. |
| Seeded scenario-driver action set | Closed | `t21.2.2` proves the named runtime scenario actions used by the approved inventory. |
| Primitive JAX-RS echo endpoints | Closed | `t21.2.2` proves text, JSON, and XML echo contracts on the seeded DB2 lane. |
| Primitive/docs/alternate-surface families as a whole | Still partial | Evidence remains selective rather than inventory-complete. |
| Remaining detailed user-visible behaviors | Still partial | The final bundle does not add focused checks for all open observable behaviors. |

## Final Product Verdict

The Spring Boot 3 rewrite is materially closer to full parity than it was in `t21.1`. The final tester rerun closes the seeded DB2 regressions that had blocked operator flows, scenario steps, and primitive JAX-RS evidence. PM sign-off still cannot pass, however, because the approved parity inventory is broader than those repaired slices, and the completed evidence bundle still stops short of proving every directly reachable docs, alternate-page, and primitive surface family plus the remaining user-visible behavior gaps. The correct product verdict remains: substantial parity achieved, but inventory-complete feature parity is not yet evidenced.
